description: Tata Steel Foundation's tribal education program aims to improve learning outcomes for children in tribal regions of Jharkhand, Odisha, and Chhattisgarh. The program supports remedial learning centers, teacher training, and infrastructure improvement in government schools. Priority given to organizations working in Schedule V areas with proven community engagement. Aligns with Schedule VII Item (ii) - promoting education.

Infosys Foundation supports programs that bring quality education and digital literacy to underserved rural communities. Focus on setting up computer labs, training teachers in ICT, and providing digital learning content in vernacular languages. The foundation has spent Rs 360 crore on education in FY2024. Aligns with Schedule VII Item (ii).
eligibilityCriteria: Registered under Societies Act or Trust Act
Track record of at least 2 years

description: HDFC Bank's Parivartan program focuses on economically empowering women through self-help groups, micro-enterprise development, and financial literacy training. The bank spent Rs 945 crore on CSR in FY2024. Programs should demonstrate sustainable livelihood creation for women in semi-urban and rural areas. Aligns with Schedule VII Items (iii) and (ii).

Reliance Foundation's health initiative focuses on improving access to primary healthcare in rural India. Programs include mobile health clinics, nutrition programs for mothers and children, training of community health workers, and support for PHCs. Reliance Industries CSR spend exceeded Rs 1,100 crore in FY2024. Aligns with Schedule VII Item (i) - healthcare.

Wipro Foundation supports environmental conservation projects focusing on water body restoration, afforestation, waste management, and biodiversity protection. Wipro spent Rs 252 crore on CSR in FY2024. Priority for projects with measurable environmental impact and community participation. Aligns with Schedule VII Item (iv) - environmental sustainability.

programName: Mahindra Pride - Skill Development for Youth
description: Mahindra's skill development initiative trains underprivileged youth for employment in automotive, IT, and service sectors. Programs include residential training, placement support, and post-placement mentoring. Mahindra Group CSR spend was Rs 290 crore in FY2024. Aligns with Schedule VII Item (ii) - employment enhancing vocational skills.

programName: Mission Sunehra Kal - Integrated Rural Development
description: ITC's rural development program creates sustainable livelihoods through integrated watershed management, sustainable agriculture, and women's empowerment in rural India. ITC spent Rs 502 crore on CSR in FY2024. Focus on creating self-sustaining rural economies. Aligns with Schedule VII Items (x) - rural development projects.
eligibilityCriteria: At least 5 years in rural areas

description: HCL Grant is one of India's largest CSR awards, providing Rs 25 lakh to 5 crore for transformative education projects. Focus on STEM education, learning outcomes improvement, and innovative pedagogy for underserved children. HCL Technologies CSR spend was Rs 270 crore in FY2024.

Adani Foundation supports community health programs including mobile health units, community health workers training, and livelihood generation in areas around Adani operations. CSR spend for Adani Group exceeded Rs 1,000 crore in FY2024. Aligns with Schedule VII Items (i) and (iii).
eligibilityCriteria: Operational near Adani project sites preferred

description: L&T's CSTI program trains rural youth in construction skills including masonry, plumbing, electrical work, and welding. Includes placement support and certification. L&T CSR spend was Rs 221 crore in FY2024. Focus on creating employable youth from economically weaker sections.
